results.title: Greek curriculum
results.creator_credit: Dimitri Man and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: The Greek Curriculum program is designed for children in Australian schools catering for the mother tongue and second language learners. The same program is repeated twice. The first has English subtitles and Greek dialogue whilst the second is in Greek only.

results.title: A Death in the family
results.creator_credit: Peter Wells, Stewart Main, James H. Wallace, New Zealand Film Commission and Television New Zealand
results.brief_description: An outstanding dramatisation of a group of gay men nursing a friend dying of AIDS in his final 16 days. This beautifully shot film addresses the private realities of living and loving against the public front of a “horror” disease.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Peter Wells, Stewart Main, James H. Wallace, New Zealand Film Commission and Television New Zealand

results.title: Extinction is forever!
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer and Victoria. Ministry of Education
results.brief_description: Kerry Aulsbrook, an Education Officer specializing in natural history at the Museum of Victoria introduces Australian customs regulations prohibiting imports of animals and natural products. Examines the threats faced by species now close to extinction because of human interference.

results.title: Beyond gravity
results.creator_credit: Garth Maxwell, James H. Wallace and James Wallace Productions
results.brief_description: Richard spends his time fantasising about outerspace and the cosmos, an activity which antagonises friends and family. Then one day he meets Johnny, a handsome Italian man, with an enormous appetite for life, who offers him love and an escape from the monotony of work. This short New Zealand film is a joyous celebration of the love between two eccentrics. It is also a refreshingly matter-of-fact and comic treatment of a romance between two men who both have their sights aimed firmly amongst the stars.

results.title: Greetings from Wollongong
results.brief_description: Four unemployed young people roam around one of Australia’s largest industrial cities. Combines social realist fiction with an essay-like examination of the city of Wollongong. This film is equally engaging and intellectually rigorous. As an exploration of a once leading steel-producing city, now increasingly blighted by unemployment, it also becomes a requiem for post-war working class Australia.

results.title: More new handwriting
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Diane O'Connor and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Three related programs by Barbara Nichol on the teaching of the new handwriting style for Victorian government schools. 1.”The New handwriting”. Provides an introduction to the new handwriting course for all members of the school community. 2.”Learning handwriting”. Highlights the reasons for implementing the course. 3.”Victorian modern cursive”. Provides a demostration of the course.

results.title: High Ground
results.brief_description: An historical overview of building development in the City of Melbourne culminating in the building of 101 Collins Street. Some fascinating archival footage is used to illustrate how the top end of Collins Street and the city in general developed as “the high ground”.
